info.name: Bit-Cert — Full API Test Suite
info.description:
Comprehensive QA collection for Bit-Cert backend.

Prerequisites:
- Backend running on {{baseUrl}} (default: http://localhost:5000)
- Hyperledger Fabric test network active
- PostgreSQL running with Prisma migrations applied
- SMTP configured (e.g. MailHog: SMTP_HOST=localhost SMTP_PORT=1025)

Newman run:
  npm install -g newman
  newman run tests/bit-cert-collection.json -e tests/bit-cert-environment.json --timeout-request 30000 --delay-request 300

Test order is sequential — variables chain across folders.

exec: pm.test('token is a non-empty JWT string', () => pm.expect(j.token).to.be.a('string').and.have.length.above(20));

item.name: 1.7 — Get Org Recipients (empty for new org)

exec: pm.test('total is 0 for brand new org', () => pm.expect(j.total).to.eql(0));

item.name: 1.8 — Get Org Certificates (empty for new org)

exec: pm.test('data is an empty array', () => { pm.expect(j.data).to.be.an('array'); pm.expect(j.data.length).to.eql(0); });
pm.test('page and limit present', () => { pm.expect(j.page).to.be.a('number'); pm.expect(j.limit).to.be.a('number'); });
pm.test('token contains a dot separator (base64url.base64url)', () => pm.expect(j.token).to.include('.'));
pm.test('message confirms activation', () => pm.expect(j.message).to.eql('Account activated. You can now log in.'));
pm.test('Status is 410 Gone (token already used)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(410));
pm.test('token is a non-empty JWT string', () => pm.expect(j.token).to.be.a('string').and.have.length.above(20));
pm.test('blockchain_tx_id is present and starts with tx_', () => pm.expect(j.blockchain_tx_id).to.be.a('string').and.match(/^tx_/));
pm.test('qr_code is a base64 PNG data URL', () => pm.expect(j.qr_code).to.be.a('string').and.match(/^data:image\/png;base64,/));
pm.test('data is a non-empty array', () => { pm.expect(j.data).to.be.an('array'); pm.expect(j.data.length).to.be.above(0); });

pm.test('first cert has required fields', () => { const c = j.data[0]; pm.expect(c.cert_hash).to.be.a('string'); pm.expect(c.course).to.be.a('string'); pm.expect(c.is_revoked).to.be.a('boolean'); pm.expect(c.recipient_name).to.be.a('string'); });
pm.test('search returned at least 1 result', () => pm.expect(j.data.length).to.be.above(0));
pm.test('result course matches search term (case-insensitive)', () => { const courses = j.data.map(c => c.course.toLowerCase()); pm.expect(courses.some(c => c.includes('blockchain'))).to.be.true; });
pm.test('all returned certs are not revoked', () => { j.data.forEach(c => pm.expect(c.is_revoked).to.be.false); });
pm.test('data is a non-empty array', () => pm.expect(j.data.length).to.be.above(0));
pm.test('recipient email in list', () => { const emails = j.data.map(r => r.email); pm.expect(emails).to.include(pm.collectionVariables.get('recipientEmail')); });
pm.test('first cert has qr_url pointing to verify endpoint', () => pm.expect(j.certificates[0].qr_url).to.include('/api/verify/'));

item.name: 3.10 — Org — Audit Log contains ISSUE entry

exec: pm.test('data has at least one entry', () => pm.expect(j.data.length).to.be.above(0));

pm.test('an ISSUE audit entry exists for this cert', () => { const entry = j.data.find(e => e.action === 'ISSUE' && e.target === pm.collectionVariables.get('certHash')); pm.expect(entry).to.be.an('object'); });

pm.test('ISSUE entry has metadata', () => { const entry = j.data.find(e => e.action === 'ISSUE' && e.target === pm.collectionVariables.get('certHash')); pm.expect(entry.metadata).to.be.an('object'); });
pm.test('reason field is absent on valid cert', () => pm.expect(j).to.not.have.property('reason'));
pm.test('valid is false for unknown hash', () => pm.expect(j.valid).to.be.false);
pm.test('cert_hash in response matches revoked cert', () => pm.expect(j.cert_hash).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('certHash')));
pm.test('valid is false after revocation', () => pm.expect(j.valid).to.be.false);
pm.test('message indicates already revoked', () => pm.expect(j.message).to.include('already revoked'));
pm.test('data is a non-empty array', () => pm.expect(j.data.length).to.be.above(0));
pm.test('all returned certs are revoked', () => { j.data.forEach(c => pm.expect(c.is_revoked).to.be.true); });
pm.test('message is generic (no credential hint)', () => pm.expect(j.message).to.eql('Invalid email or password.'));
pm.test('message is generic (prevents email enumeration)', () => pm.expect(j.message).to.eql('Invalid email or password.'));
pm.test('message is generic', () => pm.expect(j.message).to.eql('Invalid email or password.'));
// Uses a certificate ID that belongs to the org's recipient.
// When accessed by the recipient using their own token it should succeed (tested in 3.8).
// We tamper the ID to a random UUID to trigger 404, verifying ownership gate exists.
pm.test('Status is 403 or 404 (access denied or not found)', () => pm.expect([403, 404]).to.include(pm.response.code));
pm.test('Status is 401 for tampered token', () => pm.response.to.have.status(401));

item.name: 7.1 — Forgot Password — Known Org Email (silent success)

exec: pm.test('Status is 200 (response is always silent)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));

item.name: 7.2 — Forgot Password — Unknown Email (silent 200, no enumeration)

exec: pm.test('Status is 200 (silent — prevents email enumeration)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));
pm.test('success is true (indistinguishable from known email)', () => pm.expect(j.success).to.be.true);
pm.test('same message as known email (no information leak)', () => pm.expect(j.message).to.be.a('string').and.include('email'));

item.name: 8.1 — Onboard Org — Password Too Short (<10 chars) → 422
8.4 — Issue Certificate — Both recipient_id and recipient_email → 422
8.5 — Issue Certificate — Neither recipient_id nor recipient_email → 422

exec: pm.test('Status is 422 (xor — at least one required)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(422));
pm.test('Status is 400 (hash fails Joi length(64) check)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(400));

item.name: 8.7 — Org Certificates — Search with No Results

exec: pm.test('data is an empty array', () => { pm.expect(j.data).to.be.an('array'); pm.expect(j.data.length).to.eql(0); });
pm.test('limit is respected (at most 1 item returned)', () => pm.expect(j.data.length).to.be.at.most(1));
pm.test('qr_code is a PNG data URL', () => pm.expect(j.qr_code).to.match(/^data:image\/png;base64,/));
pm.test('Status is 403 Forbidden (cannot revoke another org cert)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));

item.name: 9.4 — Org2 Get Org1 Certificate by ID → 403

exec: pm.test('Status is 403 Forbidden (org2 cannot read org1 cert)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));

item.name: 9.5 — Org2 Org Stats (only sees own data, not org1 data)

exec: pm.test('total_certificates is 0 for brand new org2', () => pm.expect(j.total_certificates).to.eql(0));
pm.test('total_recipients is 0 for brand new org2', () => pm.expect(j.total_recipients).to.eql(0));

item.name: 9.6 — Org2 Certificates List (empty — scoped to org2 only)

exec: pm.test('data is empty (org2 has no certs, org1 certs not visible)', () => { pm.expect(j.data).to.be.an('array'); pm.expect(j.data.length).to.eql(0); });
